Design Brief · Confidential

TickeyBox — Packaging Design Brief

For Wendy · Version 2
v2 Updated 11 May 2026
Phase 1 ~18 May · Cape Town · 10 families
Phase 2 ~26 May · Cape Town · 10 families
Phase 3 ~9 June · Johannesburg · 10 families
Launch July 2026
Contact KB / MB

Brand Quick Reference

Colours

Warm Coral #FF6E60
Butter Yellow #FFED6B
Pastel Mint #B2E5C9
Midnight Navy #08113F
Cream Canvas #FFFAEF

Typography

  • Bungee — display headings, element names, large callouts. All caps or sentence case.
  • Poppins — subheadings, labels, UI-style copy. Medium/SemiBold.
  • Inter — body text, instructions, small print. Regular/Medium.
  • All fonts available free on Google Fonts.

Tone + Style

  • Calm, warm, nostalgic — never preachy or fear-based.
  • Curves over corners. Generous border-radius. Wave/arc dividers. Blob accents at 15–25% opacity.
  • Logo: Coral keypad icon + Bungee "TICKEYBOX" wordmark. Full lockup where space allows.
  • Illustrations: clean hand-drawn line art, navy ink on cream. No faces.
  • Pixel/mosaic pattern (Coral + Yellow + Mint + Black + White squares) is the TickeyBox packaging signature — use consistently.

Visual References — Tin Can (Seattle)

Tin Can is the closest global comparable to TickeyBox — a child-safe, parent-controlled landline system. They started with repurposed Goodwill phones and grew to bespoke custom hardware. Their packaging, unboxing experience, and cable packet naming are the primary visual reference for TickeyBox. Important: Tin Can uses fully custom-designed hardware. TickeyBox uses the Grandstream GHP611W (repurposed hospitality phone). Reference the packaging spirit and personality — not the form factor.

1 — Full Packaging System

The complete Tin Can unboxing: yellow e-flute outer box, bright yellow interior with brand mark on inside lid, patterned tissue-wrapped cable packets, and the setup welcome card. Note: the blue object is Tin Can's custom phone base unit — part of the hardware, not a packaging insert.

Full spread. Yellow box open showing phone, two tissue-wrapped cable packets, and the setup welcome card in front.
Box exterior. All-over illustrated curly cord phones + Tin Can logo. Dense pattern, brand-forward surface print.
Box interior. Yellow interior with Tin Can logo on inner partition — the 'surprise reveal' moment. Parallel to our 'HELLO.' concept.
"ITS HEEEEEEERE!" Arrival moment. Yellow box with patterned tissue visible inside, QR code on inside of lid.
2 — Cable Packets: "Twirly Cord" + "Power"

Tin Can wraps cables in patterned tissue paper — NOT kraft bags. Names ("Twirly Cord" and "Power") are printed directly on the tissue. Small sticker label on front shows cord icons. Our approach uses brown kraft bags (off-the-shelf) with a branded sticker label — same naming logic, different substrate.

Both packets. Different colourways per cable type. Icon sticker labels on front.
Name labels close-up. "Twirly Cord" (pink tissue) and "Power" (orange tissue). TickeyBox equivalent: "CURLY", "POWER", "LAN" — our names, our colourways, on brown kraft.
3 — Setup / Welcome Card

Tin Can's setup card: "Say hello to your new Tin Can." — yellow card, large QR code, 3-step setup. Becomes a semi-permanent fixture in the home. Direct reference for our quick start guide cover (element 05).

Card in context. Propped on the sideboard — the phone + coiled cord live alongside it. Becomes part of the home display.
Card in use. Yellow setup card on the floor while child makes her first call. The card travels with the child during setup.
4 — The Device (Context Only)

Tin Can's phone is custom-designed hardware — ribbed white handset + coloured base. Available in multiple bold colours. TickeyBox uses the Grandstream GHP611W. Reference the colour personality and use — not the form factor.

Custom hardware. White ribbed handset + blue branded base. The blue base is part of the phone, not packaging.
Handset alone. Ribbed body, clean Tin Can logo. Distinctive form unique to TinCan.
Pink variant in use. Phone + matching coiled cord. The curly cord is central to product identity — same for TickeyBox.
Colour range. Multiple handset variants. Strong colour identity is a strategic asset.
5 — Unboxing Reactions

The emotional experience we are designing toward. The unboxing is an event, not just opening a box. Kids encounter a real phone for the first time — confusion → delight → pride. This content gets shared. The packaging drives the reaction.

"They are baffled." First encounter — confusion before delight.
Boy unboxing. Cable packs and tissue wrapping visible inside box.
Reaching in. Coiled cables visible. The tactile discovery moment.
The box as object. Bold enough to hold on its own before opening.
First sight of the phone. Yellow box open, phone base visible. The lid-lift reveal.
Parent + child together. The unboxing is a shared ritual.
Discovery process. Tissue-wrapped packets feel like small gifts.
Inside lid smiley. Small brand detail on inside of lid. Parallel to our 'HELLO.' concept.
WSJ coverage. Decorated/personalised phone. Kids make it their own.
6 — Cultural Context + Company Story

The movement TickeyBox operates within. Tin Can started with Goodwill-acquired phones, validated demand, then invested in custom hardware — directly parallel to TickeyBox's approach with the Grandstream.

People magazine. "Parents Surprise Kids with Landlines." Mainstream validation.
Product comms style. Bold graphic, clear function explanation. Confident, colourful, no technical language.
Feature comms. How Tin Can explains features — simple, visual, parent-friendly.
Company positioning. "A screen-free phone for kids that gives them the independence to call friends and family."
Origin story. CEO: "16 months ago… the first Tin Can call, from his laptop to a Goodwill-acquired analog phone." Started scrappy — us too.
First prototype in use. Original Goodwill phone, kitchen counter. TickeyBox is at this same stage.
Early prototypes. Demand validated with Goodwill phones, then hardware investment followed.
Fast Company Most Innovative 2026. Where Tin Can is now. This is the destination.
PILOT — PHASE 1
~18 May · 2.5 weeks
Date~18 May 2026
LocationCape Town
Households10 families
Elements02 Done ✓ · 05 Done ✓ · 06 Sampling · 09 Sampling
Element 02 · Pilot only
QR Code Sticker for Box
Phase 1 Done ✓
✅

Design complete for pilot. QR sticker produced and ready for Phase 1. Links to an electronic quick start guide. This sticker is pilot-specific — it exists because we retain the OEM Grandstream box for the pilot rather than a bespoke TickeyBox box.

At launch: the QR code moves to the physical quick start guide (element 05). A launch-appropriate version of the QR sticker / reference to the quick start guide will be designed as part of element 05 — no separate sticker required at launch.

Element 05 · Pilot only
Cable Bag Stickers × 3 — Power / Curly / LAN
Phase 1 Done ✓
✅

Design complete for pilot. Three cable bag stickers — Power, Curly, LAN — designed and produced for Phase 1. Used with off-the-shelf brown kraft bags (not white/cream). The sticker is the full brand expression; the bag is plain brown kraft.

Note on launch: if the pilot approach (brown kraft bag + sticker) works well visually and operationally across all three phases, this becomes the launch solution — and bespoke cable bags (element 04) will not be needed. This is yet to be determined based on pilot results. No further design work on element 04 until after Phase 1 results are reviewed.

Element 06 · Phase 1 + Launch
A6 Telephone Directory
Phase 1 Carries to launch Sampling
🖨

Design complete. Samples in production. Brief to printer: A6 size, 300gsm uncoated stock, printed back-to-back (double-sided). This is a card, not a book. Uncoated stock is intentional — a child can write on it with any pen or pencil. Awaiting printed samples to assess before approving for the full pilot run.

Format
A6 card, double-sided. 300gsm uncoated. Print: digital press for pilot; same artwork reprinted at larger run for launch. A6 — not A5. A5 was assessed as too large for this piece.
Purpose
The child's personal phone book. Sits in the box alongside the quick start guide. Unboxing ritual: parent fills in the child's name and presents it with the phone. Child writes contacts on it. Uncoated stock makes this possible with any pen.
Pilot vs launch
Design once for Phase 1. Same artwork carries to launch unchanged. Only the print run size changes. The format may evolve to a small booklet in future, but this is unlikely given cost — the card format feels right.
Future evolution
A small bound telephone directory booklet has been considered for a future version of the product but is unlikely given cost. The A6 card format is the confirmed approach for now.

Visual Reference — 90s South African Telkom Telephone Directory

  • Think White Pages / residential telephone directory from the 90s. Official-looking, slightly bureaucratic, familiar and nostalgic. Parents recognise it. Kids feel it's something important.
  • Butter Yellow wash (~15% opacity) over cream/white stock.
  • Midnight Navy text throughout.
  • Bungee header (large, top of card): "TickeyBox Directory"
  • Poppins subheader: "Personal Edition"
  • Monospaced or typewriter-style font for contact entry lines.
  • Ruled lines for entries. Name and Number as column headers.
  • Optional: small retro telephone icon or rotary dial motif in Midnight Navy line art.
  • Must feel official enough that a child takes it seriously — not a toy notebook.

Copy — All confirmed

  • Header: TickeyBox Directory
  • Subheader: Personal Edition
  • Ownership line: This phone belongs to _______________
  • Column headers: Name and Number
  • Pre-printed emergency numbers: Police · 10111   Ambulance · 10177   Emergency · 112
  • Back face Easter eggs — to be confirmed by KB/MB. Legacy Telkom service numbers may no longer be active. Do not include until confirmed.
Element 09 · Phase 1 + Launch
Handset Slot Sticker — Replace Hotel "Room No." Card
Phase 1 Carries to launch Sampling
🖨

Two design options produced and sent to printer for samples. The approach has changed from a card insert to a sticker — the sticker fits in the handset slot rather than being a rigid card. Two design variants are being sampled. Awaiting printed samples before a final decision is made on which option to proceed with.

Format
Sticker (not a card insert) sized to fit the clear-fronted slot on the Grandstream GHP611W handset face. Exact dimensions as measured from the physical unit.
Purpose
The Grandstream handset has a hotel information window ("Room No.") on its face — the most visible spot on the device. Replacing it removes the hotel identity and puts TickeyBox branding where the eye goes first.
Two options
Two design variants are being sampled simultaneously. KB/MB to select from samples once received. No further design work required from Wendy until sample decision is made.
Pilot vs launch
Chosen design carries from Phase 1 through to launch unchanged. Same artwork, larger print run at launch.
PILOT — PHASE 2
~26 May · 4 weeks
Date~26 May 2026
LocationCape Town
Households10 families
Carries from Phase 1Elements 02 · 05 · 06 · 09
New in Phase 2Element 05 (quick start guide)
Element 05 (Guide) · Phase 2 + Launch
Quick Start Guide — A6 Folded Format with QR
Phase 2 Carries to launch Content from KB/MB needed
Format
A6 folded format — closed size A6 (148×105mm). The specific fold type (bi-fold, Z-fold, etc.) to be determined collaboratively with Wendy once design direction is clear and content scope is confirmed. 170–200gsm uncoated. Print: digital press for pilot; larger run for launch.
Purpose
A printed version of the setup content — derived from the e-version of the quick start guide (which lives online and is linked from the pilot QR sticker, element 02). The two are related but distinct: the pilot QR sticker points to the e-version; this printed guide is the physical equivalent. Will likely include a QR code linking to app setup/download, but this is a different QR code to the pilot sticker QR. Content scope and QR destination to be confirmed before Wendy can begin layout.
Phase 2 vs launch
Design once for Phase 2. Same artwork carries to launch. At launch, only the print run size changes.
Print deadline
Must be print-ready by ~19 May to hit Phase 2 delivery (~26 May). KB/MB to supply body copy ASAP — Wendy needs time to design and iterate.
⚠️

Blocked on copy. KB/MB are working on the copy — both the content scope and specific wording are still being developed. Wendy to apply her own design thinking once copy is in hand. Panel structure, visual approach, and fold type all open for Wendy's direction at that point.

CarriesAll Phase 1 + Phase 2 elements unchanged

No new design elements in Phase 3

Phase 3 carries forward all Phase 1 and Phase 2 elements unchanged. No new design work required. This phase is a reprint of the same artwork files, dispatched to Johannesburg.

  • Action required (KB/MB): Confirm print and delivery plan for JHB before ~2 June. Options: ship finished packs from Cape Town, or source a JHB digital press using the same print-ready files.
  • Wendy: Supply all final print-ready PDFs for all elements by end of May.
  • No design changes between Phase 2 and Phase 3 unless critical. Assess any Phase 1/2 feedback separately.
LAUNCH — JULY 2026
Launch
TargetJuly 2026
Carries from pilot unchangedElements 05 (guide) · 06 · 09
New at launchBespoke box + inserts (Route A or B, TBD)
ScrappedElement 07 (sticker pack)
🗑

Element 07 (Sticker Pack) has been removed from scope. Too expensive for the current phase. Not to be designed or produced. Do not include in any design planning or cost estimates.

Elements 01 + 02 · Launch
Bespoke Box + Inserts — Two Routes Under Evaluation
Launch Awaiting pricing + MOQ

Two suppliers are being evaluated simultaneously. Pricing and MOQs are pending from both. The chosen route will determine whether a sleeve is required and how inserts are designed. Both routes use off-the-shelf brown kraft bags for cables — the cable bag aesthetic must work with whichever box route is chosen.

Route A — In-the-Box
Brown kraft box · Midnight Navy print · colour sleeve
  • Box: 200×230×70mm brown kraft
  • Print: Midnight Navy ink on kraft surface
  • Inserts: phone insert + quick start guide insert, brown kraft (structural form to be confirmed with In-the-Box)
  • Sleeve: full-colour sleeve required, produced separately (see element 02 brief below)
  • Cable bags: brown kraft — consistent with entire box environment
  • Wendy designs: full-colour sleeve + any surface print on the box
Route B — Masterpack
All-white Valkraft box · optional CMYK print · no sleeve
  • Outer carton: 230×190×70mm, 450gsm Valkraft (white)
  • Phone insert: 228×50×48mm, 450gsm Valkraft (white)
  • Cover insert: 228×192mm, 450gsm Valkraft (white)
  • All three components white. Can remain white or be CMYK printed in full colour — no sleeve needed, box surface is the print canvas
  • Cable bags: brown kraft — sits in contrast to the all-white environment (see design question below)
  • Wendy designs: box surface + insert surfaces in CMYK on white Valkraft — full palette available

Design Question for Wendy — Both Routes

The cable bags are brown kraft in both routes (off-the-shelf, stickers already designed for pilot). How the bags sit aesthetically depends entirely on which box route is chosen — and the answer is different for each:

  • Route A (In-the-Box — brown kraft box + brown kraft inserts): The brown kraft cable bags are a seamless match. Box, inserts, and bags are all the same brown kraft substrate — a consistent, cohesive natural environment. The sticker labels on each bag provide the colour and naming. This route has no tension between the bag aesthetic and the box aesthetic.
  • Route B (Masterpack — all white box + all white inserts): The brown kraft cable bags will contrast against the white environment. This contrast could be intentional and attractive — a natural kraft element inside a clean white box — or it may feel inconsistent. This needs a design view: does brown kraft work as a deliberate material contrast inside an all-white Masterpack system, or should the bags be white or coloured to match?
  • Implication for element 04 (bespoke bags): If Route A is chosen, bespoke bags may not be needed — brown kraft off-the-shelf bags already fit. If Route B is chosen, bespoke bags (white substrate, or a brand colour) may be desirable to maintain a consistent all-white or designed interior. The bag decision therefore cannot be finalised until the route decision is made.
  • Request to Wendy: once the route decision is made, please advise on the bag approach that best serves the chosen box environment. No design work on bags needed until then.
⏳

Awaiting pricing and MOQ from both In-the-Box and Masterpack. Route decision cannot be made until numbers are in. Once route is confirmed: Wendy to begin structural design work on the chosen route. Box dieline (Route A) or CMYK box artwork (Route B) is the first deliverable after route decision.

Element 02 · Launch — Route A only
Bespoke Sleeve
Launch Only if Route A (In-the-Box) chosen

Status

  • The sleeve is only required if Route A (In-the-Box) is chosen. Route B (Masterpack) prints directly on the box — no sleeve.
  • Wendy can begin moodboarding and layout exploration now in parallel with the supplier evaluation — the visual direction is clear regardless of route.
  • Final sleeve dimensions are derived from the confirmed box dimensions. Do not start final artwork until box dimensions are locked.
Element 04 · Launch — On hold
Bespoke Cable Bags × 3
Launch Pending quotes + design direction

Status Note

  • Element 04 is on hold — not cancelled, but not progressing until two things are resolved.
  • Cost: Quotes are pending from both In-the-Box and Masterpack for the box and inserts. The cable bag decision is directly linked to those costs — bespoke bags add cost on top of the box system and must make financial sense in that context.
  • Conceptual fit: The cable bag design must work with whichever box and sleeve direction is chosen. Brown kraft off-the-shelf bags (pilot approach) may or may not be the right solution at launch depending on how the overall box aesthetic is resolved — whether that is a kraft-natural Route B environment or a full-colour sleeve Route A environment. Bespoke bags, if needed, would be designed to complement the chosen direction rather than be designed in isolation.
  • No design work on this element until: (1) supplier quotes received and route decision made, and (2) box/sleeve design direction established. The pilot brown kraft + sticker approach is the default unless those two inputs indicate otherwise.

Updated 11 May 2026. Phase 1 is ~18 May. Phases 1 pilot elements are done or in sampling — the primary open work is Phase 2.

📋 Phase 2 — Start now (~26 May)

~4 weeks · Cape Town

KB/MB: provide body copy for the guide. Target: content to Wendy by ~19 May so there is time to design and print before ~26 May.

🚀 Launch — July 2026

  • Cable bags (04) — likely not needed. Assess post-Phase 1.
